title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Independent Operations Trial
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
